scope: PDE, scientific-compute, physics simulation, and BuildLang/buildc proof-receipt wedge map

rows.buyer: scientific programmers, quant teams, research labs, HPC users
rows.official_positioning: High-performance dynamic programming language for technical and scientific computing, compiling to efficient native code via LLVM with reproducible environments and a large scientific ecosystem.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Julia is a mature scientific language and ecosystem; the public positioning is language/runtime oriented rather than a claim-level proof packet binding source, compiler/runtime receipts, numerical thresholds, formal proofs, and external verdicts.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc can compete by making compiler/runtime receipts first-class for every scientific claim, not only by matching language ergonomics or performance.

rows.buyer: scientific machine learning, differential equation, inverse problem, and model-discovery teams
rows.official_positioning: Composable open-source software for scientific machine learning, differential equations, nonlinear systems, inverse problems, automated model discovery, differentiable programming, and physics-informed AI.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: SciML is strong at solver breadth and differentiable modeling; Telos/Build should add portable proof packets that distinguish continuous identities, discretization assumptions, runtime receipts, and verifier verdicts.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc should target receipt-rich scientific kernels where solver outputs carry invariant checks and falsification labels by default.

rows.buyer: PDE researchers, finite-element engineers, scientific computing users
rows.official_positioning: Open-source computing platform for solving PDEs with finite element methods, translating scientific models into efficient finite-element code across laptops and HPC.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: FEniCS helps express and solve PDEs; Telos/Build can wrap runs with claim semantics, invariant checks, source/workspace hashes, and Crucible verdicts.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: Use FEniCS-like problems as benchmark packets for BuildLang/buildc PDE receipt design.

rows.buyer: finite-element researchers, simulation engineers, PDE method developers
rows.official_positioning: Automated system for solving PDEs with finite element methods, using code generation to create high-productivity high-performance simulations.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Firedrake exposes sophisticated PDE generation and solvers; Telos/Build can add auditable proof packets that explain which mathematical invariant, discretization, mesh, and solver receipt supports a claim.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc should produce Firedrake-style high-performance kernels with attached proof receipts and negative stability probes.

rows.buyer: HPC scientific-computing teams, PDE framework builders, solver developers
rows.official_positioning: Portable, Extensible Toolkit for Scientific Computation for scalable parallel solution of scientific applications modeled by PDEs, with MPI, GPU, C, Fortran, and Python bindings.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: PETSc is infrastructure for scalable solver execution; Telos/Build should consume solver metadata and attach claim-level thresholds, invariant probes, and reproducibility receipts.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc can target PETSc interoperability while making solver receipts part of the language/runtime contract.

rows.buyer: CFD engineers, government labs, academia, industrial simulation teams
rows.official_positioning: Free open-source CFD software written in C++, used for fluid motion, forces, heat transfer, thermodynamics, and chemistry in fluids and solids.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: OpenFOAM gives open CFD source and customization; Telos/Build can add verification packets for case setup, boundary conditions, solver version, mesh quality, invariants, and decision claims.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc can win early trust by exporting OpenFOAM-style case receipts and invariant checks instead of only presenting benchmark speed.

rows.buyer: engineering simulation, digital twin, CFD/structural/electromagnetic AI teams
rows.official_positioning: Open-source Python framework for building, training, and fine-tuning physics AI models at scale, including surrogate models, neural operators, GNNs, digital twins, and PDE-enabled training pipelines.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: PhysicsNeMo targets physics AI model creation and deployment; Telos/Build should add evidence packets for what physics invariant, training receipt, validation set, and uncertainty bound makes an output credible.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc can become the deterministic receipt layer underneath physics AI demos, especially when surrogate outputs need invariant checks.

rows.buyer: engineers and scientists modeling coupled physical systems
rows.official_positioning: Simulation platform for real-world designs, devices, and processes with fully coupled multiphysics and single-physics modeling, model builder, application builder, and model manager.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: COMSOL organizes models and applications, but Telos/Build can create external proof packets that make assumptions, invariants, solver settings, and decision claims portable outside the commercial project file.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc should export compact proof kits for claims derived from multiphysics runs, interoperating with commercial simulation rather than replacing it immediately.

rows.buyer: CFD, aerospace, automotive, energy, electronics cooling, and industrial simulation teams
rows.official_positioning: General-purpose CFD software for fluid flow, heat and mass transfer, chemical reactions, advanced physics, multiphysics integrations, optimization, and scalable CPU/GPU solving.
rows.proof_receipt_gap: Fluent is mature and broadly used; Telos/Build can differentiate at the review boundary by packaging case assumptions, exported data, solver settings, and invariant checks as independent proof packets.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc proof kits should target Fluent-adjacent workflows where decisions require auditable evidence outside a GUI-driven simulation environment.

rows.buyer: engineering teams needing cloud-native simulation and design exploration
rows.official_positioning: AI-native cloud engineering simulation platform combining Engineering AI, Physics AI, multiphysics solvers, workflow orchestration, simulation at cloud scale, collaboration, and traceability.
capabilities: AI agents for setup and validation
rows.proof_receipt_gap: SimScale emphasizes cloud-scale simulation and traceability; Telos/Build can focus on portable claim packets that move across local, cloud, open-source, and commercial simulations.
rows.buildlang_wedge_hypothesis: BuildLang/buildc should treat SimScale-style workflows as evidence sources and compete on open proof receipt portability.
